system.description: The Retiarius Style gets its name from ancient gladiators who fought with the net and trident. The gladiators were meant to emulate a fisherman, and their traditional opponent wore scaly armor and a helmet resembling a fish. Though this style of fighting has long since fallen out of fashion, it is not completely forgotten, and talented warriors can be found using it in coastal villages. A fighter of the Retiarius Style learns to use the net defensively while also maximizing use of the trident. Because it requires the trident be wielded in one hand, a Retiarius should have a Might of 1 or greater.

benefit.description: When you successfully use the net to make the Cloak Parry maneuver, you can use the trident to make the Riposte maneuver with a Bonus Die. You also get +1 when using the net to Feint.
benefit.name: Flourish and Stab

finalSecret.description: Your mastery of the net increases the range increment to 10’. If you get a Mighty Success when using the net to use Dirty Fighting, the opponent loses their next turn before they can try to escape your net.
finalSecret.name: Master of the Net
